question.text: What is the formula for calculating the number of equity index futures contracts to change portfolio beta from its current level to a target beta?

answer.text: (Target Beta - Current Beta) × Portfolio Value / (Futures Price × Lot Size)
Target Beta × Portfolio Value / (Futures Price × Lot Size)
(Current Beta - Target Beta) × Portfolio Value / (Futures Price × Lot Size)
Current Beta × Target Beta × Portfolio Value / (Futures Price × Lot Size)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** To change beta: N = (βT - βC) × V / (F × L). If target > current, N is positive (buy futures to increase beta). If target < current, N is negative (sell futures to decrease beta). If target = 0, the formula reduces to the full hedge formula: -βC × V / (F × L). **The Trap:** The formula uses the difference in betas, not a product. Using (Current - Target) instead of (Target - Current) would reverse the trade direction. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch5 covers beta modification using futures. Series IV has an analogous formula for changing portfolio duration using bond futures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** You can also use futures to INCREASE portfolio beta (leverage) — buy futures to synthetically increase exposure. This is cheaper than buying additional stocks.
